keyframes.citation: Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Pickett's start line just south of the Virginia Memorial / east edge of Spangler's Woods); strength 1,480 present: Stone Sentinels Garnett's Brigade page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: Rawley Martin, SHSP v32 (regiment-by-regiment, Garnett left)
interpolated: line static in the swale under two-hour bombardment (Alexander 1907; Haskell 1908 'two mortal hours'); small counter-battery losses reconstructed

step-off: modern reconstruction ~3:00 PM (ABT map set; Haskell 1908 has the cannonade end 'at three o'clock almost precisely'); Alexander 1907 says ~1:50 and Jacobs 1864 says 2:30 — witnesses disagree by over an hour
reconstruction: reaches the Emmitsburg Road fences ~15:16 (ABT Pickett's Charge 3:30-3:45 PM map; Codori farm anchor OSM way 779833686); pace 1.36 m/s
reconstruction: formation-dress pause at the road fences ~15:16-15:18 (ABT maps); losses to canister while halted
Stone Sentinels Garnett's Brigade page: Garnett shot dead roughly 20 yards from the Angle; Bachelder Third Day sheet (High Water Mark); time ~15:23 inferred from step-off reconstruction
reconstruction: brigade shattered at the stone wall, mixed with Armistead's men (Rawley Martin SHSP v32; Haskell 1908)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map): survivors stream back toward Seminary Ridge
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
end strength 1,480 minus 941 losses: Stone Sentinels Garnett's Brigade page; ~60% Confederate loss per Longstreet 1896 p.315; survivors rally behind Seminary Ridge (reconstruction)

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Pickett's right, near Spangler's Woods); Kemper on the division right per Rawley Martin SHSP v32; strength 1,575 present: Stone Sentinels Kemper's Brigade page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: Rawley Martin, SHSP v32 (regiment-by-regiment, Kemper right)
interpolated: line static under bombardment (Alexander 1907); small counter-battery losses reconstructed
step-off: modern reconstruction ~3:00 PM (ABT map set; Haskell-implied); Alexander 1907 ~1:50 and Jacobs 1864 2:30 disagree — recorded, not reconciled
reconstruction: crosses the Emmitsburg Road just south of the Codori barn (Codori anchor OSM way 779833686; ABT 3:30-3:45 PM map; Kemper's line of advance passed the Codori buildings); pace 1.31 m/s
reconstruction: formation-dress pause at the road fences ~15:16-15:18 (ABT maps)
reconstruction: obliquing left toward the Angle while taking flank fire from Stannard's wheeled Vermont regiments (Sturtevant 1910 pp.304-305: Pickett's command 'not sixty yards away')

Stone Sentinels Kemper's Brigade page: Kemper badly wounded near the Union line south of the Angle, briefly captured, rescued by Sgt. Blanton; Bachelder Third Day sheet; time ~15:24 inferred from step-off reconstruction
reconstruction: right of the assault crushed between front and Stannard's flank fire (Sturtevant 1910; Stone Sentinels 1st NY Battery B page: Kemper's Virginians briefly overran Rorty's guns)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
end strength 1,575 minus 731 losses: Stone Sentinels Kemper's Brigade page; survivors rally behind Seminary Ridge (reconstruction)

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (second line behind Garnett/Kemper; Rawley Martin SHSP v32 places Armistead 'in rear and center', some modern accounts left-rear — recorded, not reconciled); strength 1,650 present: Stone Sentinels Armistead's Brigade page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: Rawley Martin, SHSP v32 (regiment-by-regiment, Armistead rear-center)
interpolated: second line static under bombardment (Alexander 1907)
step-off in support ~15:05: modern reconstruction (ABT map set); Alexander 1907 ~1:50 and Jacobs 1864 2:30 disagree on the hour
reconstruction: crosses the Emmitsburg Road ~200m behind the first line ~15:18 (ABT 3:30-3:45 PM map); pace 1.24 m/s including fence delay
reconstruction: closes on the wall at the Angle through the wreck of Garnett's line (Rawley Martin SHSP v32)

Rawley Martin, SHSP v32 pp.183-195: Armistead 'scaled the wall' crying 'Boys, give them the cold steel!' and fell mortally wounded under the captured guns; Fuger via NPS Hartwig 2012 (Armistead leapt the wall into Cushing's battery); time ~15:25 per NPS/modern reconstruction
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map): breakthrough sealed by Webb's and Hall's counterattack; survivors fall back
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
end strength 1,650 minus 1,191 losses: Stone Sentinels Armistead's Brigade page, corroborated by Col. W.R. Aylett's July 12, 1863 report (via Encyclopedia Virginia)

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Pettigrew's right, north of the Virginia Memorial); brigade of direction for the whole assault per Fry, SHSP v7 pp.91-93; strength 1,048 present: Stone Sentinels Archer's Brigade page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Fry's Brigade regiment list, order as listed there — not independently attested)
interpolated: line static under bombardment (Alexander 1907)
step-off: modern reconstruction ~3:00 PM (ABT map set); both divisions dressed on Fry's command (Fry SHSP v7); Alexander ~1:50 / Jacobs 2:30 disagree on the hour
reconstruction: crosses the Emmitsburg Road converging from the north so both fronts arrive together (ABT 3:30-3:45 PM map); pace 1.17 m/s including fence crossing

Fry, SHSP v7 pp.91-93: 'All of the five regimental colors of my command reached the line of the enemy's works' just north of the Angle (against Virginia-centric accounts that Pettigrew's men hung back — disagreement recorded); Fry wounded and captured at the wall; time ~15:23 inferred
reconstruction: brigade wrecked at the wall alongside Garnett's left (Fry SHSP v7)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
Stone Sentinels Archer's Brigade page: 'less than 400 men remained at the end of July 3rd' (1,048 present minus 677 losses = 371); position behind Seminary Ridge reconstructed

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Pettigrew's line north of Fry); strength 'about 2,000 present on the first day': Stone Sentinels Pettigrew's Brigade page — CAVEAT: July 1 figure, overstates July 3 strength after first-day losses (flagged, no sourced July 3 figure exists); bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Marshall's/Pettigrew's Brigade regiment list, order as listed there — not independently attested)
interpolated: line static under bombardment (Alexander 1907)
step-off: modern reconstruction ~3:00 PM (ABT map set); Alexander ~1:50 / Jacobs 2:30 disagree on the hour
reconstruction: crosses the Emmitsburg Road under canister and rifle fire from Hays's line (ABT 3:30-3:45 PM map); pace 1.20 m/s including fence crossing

reconstruction: front reaches the wall south of the Bryan barn (Fry SHSP v7 insists Pettigrew's front fought to the wall; modern scholarship partially supports); Col. Marshall killed in the advance (Stone Sentinels)
reconstruction: repulsed at the wall by Smyth's and Sherrill's fire (ABT maps)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
end strength 2,000 minus 1,405 losses (whole-battle figures): Stone Sentinels Pettigrew's Brigade page — endpoints sourced, but both include July 1 fighting; per-day split not documented

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Pettigrew's left-center); strength 'about 2,000 present on the first day': Stone Sentinels Davis's Brigade page — CAVEAT: July 1 figure, overstates July 3 strength after heavy first-day losses; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Davis's Brigade regiment list, order as listed there — not independently attested)
interpolated: line static under bombardment (Alexander 1907)
step-off: modern reconstruction ~3:00 PM (ABT map set); Alexander ~1:50 / Jacobs 2:30 disagree on the hour
reconstruction: crosses the Emmitsburg Road angling toward the Bryan farm front (ABT 3:30-3:45 PM map); pace 1.22 m/s including fence crossing
reconstruction: advance stalls and breaks short of the wall under Hays's massed rifle fire and Woodruff's canister from Ziegler's Grove (ABT maps; Bryan farm anchor OSM way 451707950)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
end strength 2,000 minus 1,397 losses (whole-battle figures): Stone Sentinels Davis's Brigade page — both endpoints include July 1 fighting; per-day split not documented

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (far left of Pettigrew's line); strength '800 muskets' (~880 officers and men): Col. R.M. Mayo's Aug. 13, 1863 report, cited via NPS Gettysburg Seminar essay 5 (secondary hop flagged; Stone Sentinels page for this brigade is a known duplication artifact and is not used); bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Brockenbrough's Brigade regiment list, order as listed there — not independently attested)
interpolated: line static under bombardment (Alexander 1907)
step-off: modern reconstruction ~3:00 PM (ABT map set); Alexander ~1:50 / Jacobs 2:30 disagree on the hour

the break itself is documented — Fry, SHSP v7 (Brockenbrough's brigade broke early on the assault's left, corroborated by modern scholarship) — but the ~15:12 timing and this position are inferred reconstruction
reconstruction: falls back to Seminary Ridge without reaching the Emmitsburg Road (Fry SHSP v7; modern scholarship)
reconstruction: rallying at the start line (ABT maps)
no reliable casualty figure located for this brigade (research pass, oob-strengths doc); light losses reconstructed from the early break

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Trimble's second line behind Pettigrew's left-center); strength 1,355 present: Stone Sentinels Lane's Brigade page (monument inscription; a conflicting 1,730 extraction of the same page exists — direct-fetch figure used, conflict flagged); bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Lane's Brigade regiment list, order as listed there — not independently attested)
interpolated: second line static under bombardment (Alexander 1907)
step-off in support of Pettigrew: modern reconstruction ~3:00 PM (ABT map set); Alexander ~1:50 / Jacobs 2:30 disagree on the hour
reconstruction: crosses the Emmitsburg Road behind Pettigrew's wrecked front (ABT 3:30-3:45 PM map); pace 1.21 m/s including fence crossing
reconstruction: pushes to within yards of the wall near the Bryan barn before the assault collapses (ABT maps; Trimble wounded here per Encyclopedia Virginia)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)

end strength 1,355 minus 660 losses: Stone Sentinels Lane's Brigade page (monument figures; page narrative separately claims Lane+Lowrance combined could field only ~800 on July 3 — conflict flagged, not reconciled)

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Trimble's second line behind Fry); strength 'about 1,250 present': Stone Sentinels Scales's Brigade page — CAVEAT: brigade had lost heavily July 1 (Wikipedia: nearly two-thirds), figure likely overstates July 3 strength; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Lowrance's/Scales's Brigade regiment list, order as listed there — not independently attested)
interpolated: second line static under bombardment (Alexander 1907)
step-off in support: modern reconstruction ~3:00 PM (ABT map set); Alexander ~1:50 / Jacobs 2:30 disagree on the hour
reconstruction: crosses the Emmitsburg Road behind Fry (ABT 3:30-3:45 PM map); pace 1.19 m/s including fence crossing; Col. Lowrance wounded in the charge (Stone Sentinels)
reconstruction: closes toward the wall north of the Angle as the front collapses (ABT maps)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
reconstruction of the repulse (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
end strength 1,250 minus 599 losses: Stone Sentinels Scales's Brigade page (whole-battle figures; Trimble's command combined lost 885 / 52% per Encyclopedia Virginia — not split by brigade)

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (echelon on Pickett's right, south of Spangler's Woods); strength 1,777 present: Stone Sentinels Wilcox's Brigade page (monument inscription; a conflicting 1,725 extraction of the same page exists — flagged); bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Wilcox's Brigade regiment list, order as listed there — not independently attested)
interpolated: line static under bombardment (Alexander 1907)
belated supporting advance steps off ~15:44: inferred timing (modern reconstruction; Wilcox advanced roughly 20 minutes after Pickett, too late to support the repulsed column)
reconstruction: advances ~590m into the open (1.09 m/s), taking artillery and flank fire from Stannard's Vermonters wheeled south (Stone Sentinels 3rd Brig 3rd Div I Corps page)
reconstruction: withdrawal ordered ~15:55 with no support on either flank (Stone Sentinels Wilcox's Brigade narrative; ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
end strength: Wilcox reported losses of ~200 in the July 3 advance (Wikipedia 'Pickett's Charge', uncited there; his whole-battle monument total is 781 — conflict flagged, July-3-only reading used)

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (echelon on Wilcox's left-rear, far right of the supports); strength 700 present: Stone Sentinels Perry's Brigade page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Lang's/Perry's Brigade regiment list, order as listed there — not independently attested)
interpolated: line static under bombardment (Alexander 1907)
steps off with Wilcox ~15:44: inferred timing (modern reconstruction)
reconstruction: advances ~580m (1.07 m/s) under flank fire from Stannard's 16th/14th Vermont wheeled south (Stone Sentinels 3rd Brig 3rd Div I Corps page)
reconstruction: withdraws with Wilcox ~15:55 (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)

end strength: 'about 400' casualties per Wikipedia 'Pickett's Charge' (uncited there; Stone Sentinels whole-battle total is 455 — conflict flagged; July-3-leaning reading used); Lang's own fate on July 3 not located in any source

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (at the Angle and the Copse; 69th/71st/72nd PA per Haskell 1908); strength 1,250: Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Webb's Brigade regiment list, incl. 106th PA)
interpolated: holding under the bombardment's concentric fire on Gibbon's division (Waitt 1906 pp.234-237)
reconstruction: holding the wall as Pickett's front closes (Haskell 1908)
Haskell 1908: the Angle crisis — a rebel flag 'already waving over one of the guns of the dead Cushing'; Webb wounded (Medal of Honor citation); time ~15:25 per NPS/modern reconstruction
reconstruction: breakthrough sealed, wall retaken (Haskell 1908; ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
end strength 1,250 minus 490 losses: Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 ('69'Pa' bar at the stone wall between the Angle and the Copse; position class, time inferred); strength 258 at the Angle: Stone Sentinels 69th Pennsylvania page; roster and company detail: McDermott 1889; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
prone at the wall under the bombardment's concentric fire on Gibbon's front (McDermott 1889; Waitt 1906 pp.234-237)
holds fire at the wall as Pickett's front closes (McDermott 1889; Haskell 1908)

the regiment never leaves the wall: Cos. I and A change front against the flankers as Armistead crosses, Co. F's captain killed before he could give the command, Co. D 'held the enemy at bay, using their muskets as clubs' (McDermott 1889 verbatim); time ~15:25 inferred
wall held, breakthrough sealed (McDermott 1889; Haskell 1908)
end: brigade losses 490 (Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table) not split by regiment — decline reconstructed

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 ('71'Pa' bar at the Angle wall; position class, time inferred); strength 331 brought to the field: Stone Sentinels 71st Pennsylvania page; 8 companies at the wall, 2 fifty yards right-rear (Smith's OR report via Stone Sentinels — the report names no companies); bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
under the bombardment at the outer-angle wall (Smith's OR report; Waitt 1906 pp.234-237 for the fire on this front)
at the wall as the column closes; ~50 men help serve Cushing's guns (Smith's OR report; NPS Hartwig 2012)
step one of the two-step: falls back from the outer angle at the breakthrough rather than be overrun (Smith's OR report via Stone Sentinels); crest-ward coordinate and time ~15:25 inferred
step two: returns to the wall as the breakthrough is sealed (Smith's OR report; Haskell 1908)
end: brigade losses 490 not split by regiment — decline reconstructed

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 ('72'Pa' bar in reserve behind the Angle crest; position class, time inferred); strength 458 'present at Gettysburg' vs 473 'present for duty' — both on Stone Sentinels 72nd Pennsylvania page, conflict kept; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
in reserve behind the crest under the bombardment (Haskell 1908)

CONTESTED — tracked reading: brought up to the crest and fires from it, not advancing when Webb orders the charge (Webb wounded trying to lead it; Haskell 1908; Medal of Honor citation for Webb); the regiment's monument-litigation counter-reading holds it fought at the wall from the start (72nd PA monument case via HistoryNet) — counter-reading recorded, not adopted; time ~15:25 inferred
the final advance to the wall as the penetration collapses ~15:35 (Haskell 1908; survey: advanced only at the end); clock inferred
end at the retaken wall (reconstruction); brigade losses 490 not split by regiment

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (immediately south of the Copse of Trees); strength 920: Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Hall's Brigade regiment list)
interpolated: prostrate under the bombardment (Waitt 1906 pp.234-237, 19th MA of this brigade)
reconstruction: regiments shift right/north into the melee at the Copse (Haskell 1908)
reconstruction: counterattack seals the breakthrough (Haskell 1908; ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
end strength 920 minus 365 losses: Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Union regiments drawn individually; the 19th MA in reserve behind Hall's line per Waitt 1906; position class, time inferred); strength 231 brought to the field: Stone Sentinels 19th Massachusetts page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
prostrate under the bombardment — Rorty's battery mauled in its front (Waitt 1906 pp.234-237)

the plunge: Devereux asks Hancock's leave and throws the 19th MA and 42nd NY into the gap at the Copse (Devereux's OR report, OR 27/1; Waitt 1906 ch. XXIX); melee-zone position — widest uncertainty (survey disagreement 9); clock ~15:25-15:30 inferred
hand-to-hand at the clump until the penetration collapses (Waitt 1906)
end: brigade losses 365 not split by regiment

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 ('20'Ms' bar, left of Hall's wall line; position class, time inferred); strength 301 brought to the field, losing 30 killed / 94 wounded / 3 missing across the battle: Stone Sentinels 20th Massachusetts page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
prostrate under the bombardment (Waitt 1906 pp.234-237)

crowds right toward the Copse with the 7th MI (Hall's OR report, OR 27/1); melee-zone position — widest uncertainty (survey disagreement 9: even Hess's 20th MA / 59th NY placement is critiqued); clock ~15:25 inferred
in the melee at the clump until the penetration collapses (Hall's OR report; Haskell 1908)
end: brigade losses 365 not split by regiment; the 20th's own whole-battle loss was 127 (Stone Sentinels)

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 ('7'Mich' bar, center of Hall's wall line; position class, time inferred); strength 165 (14 officers, 151 men present for duty): Stone Sentinels 7th Michigan page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
prostrate under the bombardment (Waitt 1906 pp.234-237)
Hall shifts his regiments right/north toward the Copse at the break (Hall's OR report, OR 27/1; Haskell 1908); melee-zone position — widest uncertainty (survey disagreement 9); clock ~15:25 inferred
close-range fight west of the Copse until the penetration collapses (Hall's OR report; Haskell 1908)
end: brigade losses 365 not split by regiment

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Union regiments drawn individually; the 42nd NY in reserve with the 19th MA behind Hall's line per Waitt 1906; position class, time inferred); strength 197 brought to the field: Stone Sentinels 42nd New York page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
prostrate under the bombardment (Waitt 1906 pp.234-237)

plunges into the gap at the Copse beside the 19th MA on Devereux's initiative with Hancock's verbal order (Devereux's OR report, OR 27/1; Waitt 1906 ch. XXIX); melee-zone position — widest uncertainty (survey disagreement 9); clock inferred
in the melee at the clump until the penetration collapses (Waitt 1906; Haskell 1908)
end: brigade losses 365 not split by regiment

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 ('59'N.Y.' bar at the wall immediately south of the Copse; position class, time inferred); a four-company battalion of 182 men, consolidated June 1863 — short frontage encoded (Stone Sentinels 59th New York page); bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
prostrate under the bombardment (Waitt 1906 pp.234-237, this brigade's front)
at the wall as Kemper's and Armistead's fronts close (Hall's OR report, OR 27/1)

the bolt: the battalion 'inexplicably bolted for the rear' at the climax, uncovering Cowan's guns — the moment of Cowan's double canister (Stone Sentinels 59th New York page; Brown 1985 Filson); formation routed; time ~15:25 and rally coordinate inferred; melee-zone positions carry the survey's widest uncertainty (disagreement 9)
rallied and returned to the line after the repulse (Hall's OR report; reconstruction)
end: brigade losses 365 (Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table) not split by regiment

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Gibbon's left, south of Hall); strength 1,410: Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table — CAVEAT: engaged figure for the whole battle; brigade lost heavily July 2 (1st MN); bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Harrow's Brigade regiment list)
interpolated: holding under bombardment (Waitt 1906)
reconstruction: regiments crowd north toward the Copse to meet the breakthrough (Haskell 1908)
reconstruction: close-range fight at the Copse (Haskell 1908; ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
strength from Stone Sentinels table (780 base) minus casualties; note War Dept marker (768) vs Harrow's own report (722) disagree by 46; totals include July 2

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Hays's line north of the Angle, toward the Bryan farm); strength 1,190: Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864; Smyth's own report puts the cannonade start at 2:00 PM — disagreement recorded); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Smyth's Brigade regiment list)
interpolated: holding under bombardment; Smyth wounded in the face by a shell fragment July 3 (Wikipedia Thomas A. Smyth)
reconstruction: massed rifle fire repulses Marshall's and Lane's front at the wall (ABT maps)
reconstruction: line holds throughout (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
end strength 1,190 minus 365 losses: Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (Hays's right, Bryan farm toward Ziegler's Grove; Bryan house anchor OSM way 451707950); strength 1,510: Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table; Willard killed July 2, Sherrill commanding; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (Sherrill's/Willard's Brigade regiment list)
interpolated: holding under bombardment (Alexander 1907)

Stone Sentinels 3rd Brigade 3rd Division 2nd Corps page: Col. Sherrill mortally wounded during the repulse of Pickett's Charge (died July 4); position at the Bryan farm line per Bachelder sheet; time ~15:25 inferred
reconstruction: line holds; Lt. Col. Bull assumes command (Stone Sentinels)
end strength 1,510 minus 710 losses: Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table (includes July 2 fighting against Barksdale)

on the advanced picket line in the sunken Emmitsburg road cut, ~200 yds in front of Hays's main line, held from July 2 (Sawyer 1881, A Military History of the 8th Reg't Ohio Vol. Inf'y, full text verified; ABT 'The Picket Line Against Pickett's Charge': 209 men); Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 ('8'Ohio' bar); Sawyer clocks the cannonade opening 'between twelve and one o'clock' and lasting 'nearly two hours' — against the 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864) anchors, disagreement kept, not reconciled; road-cut coordinate inferred from the road trace northwest of the Bryan farm

'We changed our front, and taking position by a fence, facing the left flank of the advancing column of rebels, the men were ordered to fire into their flank at will' (Sawyer 1881; Sawyer's report, OR 27/1:461-62) — the flank fire under which Brockenbrough's command broke; fence coordinate north of the column's path and time ~15:10 inferred

advanced and cut off three regiments of the column's left, taking the colors of the 34th North Carolina (Lowrance's) and the 38th Virginia (Armistead's) (Sawyer 1881; OR 27/1:461-62); time ~15:25 inferred; strength decline reconstructed — no July-3-only casualty split located

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (advanced position southwest of the main line, south of the Copse); strength 1,950: Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table — CAVEAT: only 13th/14th/16th VT engaged July 3, which sum to 1,788 (~160-man gap flagged, not reconciled); bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864); roster: docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md (13th/14th/16th VT — the three regiments engaged July 3; 12th/15th VT omitted as not engaged)
interpolated: holding the advanced line under bombardment (Sturtevant 1910)
reconstruction: Pickett's right passes across the brigade's front as it obliques toward the Copse (Sturtevant 1910; ABT 3:30-3:45 PM map)
order + flank fire documented (Sturtevant 1910 pp.304-305); regimental split per Stone Sentinels brigade page (secondary, not in verified excerpts)
reconstruction: regiments return toward the original front after Pickett's repulse (Sturtevant 1910)
reconstruction: 16th and 14th VT wheel south against Wilcox and Lang's advance (Stone Sentinels 3rd Brig 3rd Div I Corps page; Stannard wounded in the thigh directing the flank attack)
end strength 1,950 minus 350 losses: Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 ('13'Vt' regiment bar on Stannard's advanced line — the map gives position classes, not clocks; time inferred); coordinate = the regiment's RegimentSlots share of the brigade line, right of the 14th; strength 480 'officers and men engaged': 13th VT monument via Stone Sentinels; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
holding the advanced line under the bombardment (Sturtevant 1910); slot-follow of the brigade track
Pickett's right passes the brigade front obliquing north toward the Copse (Sturtevant 1910; ABT 3:30-3:45 PM map — ABT clock frame noted, never mixed silently)

wheel under way ~15:20: 'change front forward on first company' — the first company pivots, successive companies wheel in (Sturtevant 1910 pp.304-305; Benedict, Vermont in the Civil War vol. 2); clock inferred from the step-off reconstruction

13th VT monument: 'changed front forward on first company, advanced 200 yards, attacking the Confederate right flank ... capturing 243 prisoners' (Stone Sentinels 13th Vermont page); Sturtevant 1910 pp.304-305 — Pickett's command 'not sixty yards away'; coordinate = ~200 yds forward of the Bachelder line onto Kemper's right; time ~15:25 inferred
returns toward the original front after Pickett's repulse (Sturtevant 1910); slot-follow of the brigade track
end at the advanced line (reconstruction); brigade losses 350 (Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table) are not split by regiment — per-regiment decline reconstructed

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 ('14'Vt' bar, center of Stannard's advanced line; position class, time inferred); strength 647 brought to the field: Stone Sentinels 14th Vermont page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
holding under the bombardment (Sturtevant 1910); slot-follow of the brigade track
essentially static through the crisis, firing into Kemper's front as the assault obliques past (Sturtevant 1910; Stone Sentinels 3rd Brig 3rd Div I Corps page)

front refused south from its position, joining the 16th's fire against Wilcox and Lang's advance (Stone Sentinels 3rd Brig 3rd Div I Corps page; Veazey's OR report names the 16th's movement — the 14th joins by fire, not movement); clock ~15:50 inferred
end (reconstruction); brigade losses 350 (Stone Sentinels Strength & Casualties USA table) not split by regiment

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 ('16'Vt' bar, left of Stannard's advanced line; position class, time inferred); strength 661 brought to the field: Stone Sentinels 16th Vermont page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
holding under the bombardment (Sturtevant 1910); slot-follow of the brigade track
wheels out with the 13th ~15:20, forming on its left for the flank attack (Sturtevant 1910 pp.304-305; Veazey's OR report); clock inferred
on the 13th's left firing into the right flank of Pickett's column (Sturtevant 1910; Veazey's OR report); flank-position coordinate inferred from the 13th's documented 200-yd advance
recalled and re-forms facing south as Wilcox and Lang step off ~15:45 — change of front to the rear (Veazey's OR report; Benedict vol. 2); clock inferred

the 16th's reversal: strikes the left flank of Wilcox and Lang's supporting advance (Veazey's OR report; Stone Sentinels 3rd Brig 3rd Div I Corps page — Stannard wounded in the thigh directing the flank attack); time ~15:53 and coordinate inferred from Lang's track
returns to the line (reconstruction); brigade losses 350 not split by regiment

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (at the Angle; wall-corner anchor per Stone Sentinels The Angle page); strength '126 men and six cannons': US Army Medal of Honor page for Cushing; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)

reconstruction: battery wrecked by the bombardment — guns disabled, Cushing wounded in shoulder and abdomen but refusing the rear (Fuger 1904 manuscript; NPS Hartwig 2012; Webb's report has Cushing wounded much earlier and functioning longer than Fuger implies — conflict flagged)
reconstruction: remaining guns run down to the wall, firing double canister as the assault closes (Fuger via NPS Hartwig 2012)

Cushing killed ~15:20-15:25 — shot through the mouth per Fuger (1904 manuscript; JMSI 1907 via NPS Hartwig 2012), 'through the head' per Haskell 1908 (competing detail, not reconciled); Armistead's men land among the guns (Fuger, verified via NPS quote); casualties 38 per Stone Sentinels battery page
reconstruction: position retaken as the breakthrough is sealed; battery effectively destroyed (NPS Hartwig 2012)
end strength 126 minus 38 casualties: Stone Sentinels battery page (1 officer + 5 men killed, 1 officer + 31 men wounded)

units.name: Rorty's Battery B, 1st New York Light Artillery

keyframes.citation: Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (~250m south of the Copse, in front of Hall's/Harrow's sector); strength 114: Stone Sentinels 1st NY Battery B page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)

Waitt 1906 pp.234-237 (19th MA history): Rorty's battery in front of the regiment mauled during the cannonade — horses and drivers down, guns rolled helpless, a caisson burst; strength decline reconstructed from that account
Stone Sentinels 1st NY Battery B page: Capt. Rorty killed when Kemper's Virginians briefly overran the battery in hand-to-hand fighting; time ~15:25 inferred from the assault reconstruction
reconstruction: guns recovered as Kemper's men are driven off (ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map)
end strength 114 minus 26 casualties (10 killed, 16 wounded): Stone Sentinels 1st NY Battery B page

units.name: Arnold's Battery A, 1st Rhode Island Light Artillery

keyframes.citation: Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 (at the wall north of the Angle, on Smyth's front); strength 139: Stone Sentinels battery page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
interpolated: served under the bombardment (Alexander 1907)
reconstruction: canister into Fry's and Marshall's front at the wall (ABT maps)

end strength 139 minus 28 casualties (4 killed, 24 wounded): Stone Sentinels battery page; Arnold survived unwounded, 'gallantly fighting his own battery and saving it' per Hazard's report (via Iron Brigader)

July 3 start position south of its later post (Brown 1985 Filson: Cowan's battery was brought up from the south at Webb's wave; exact start coordinate inferred); strength 113: Stone Sentinels 1st NY Independent Battery page; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
limbers to move as Brown's wrecked Battery B pulls out of the line mid-bombardment (Brown 1985 Filson); clock ~14:44 inferred

the gallop-in: Cowan brings the battery up at a gallop into the position Brown's Battery B vacated just south of the Copse, waved in by Webb (Kent Masterson Brown, 'Double Canister at Ten Yards', Filson Club History Quarterly 59:3, 1985); Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 names 'COWAN' here — the map records this final position (position class; time ~14:45 inferred)
interpolated: served under the bombardment (Alexander 1907)
Stone Sentinels 1st NY Independent Battery page / Wikipedia Andrew Cowan: the 'double canister at ten yards' repulse at the Copse as Armistead's men crossed the wall; time ~15:25 inferred
end strength 113 minus 12 casualties (4 killed, 8 wounded): Stone Sentinels battery page

units.name: Brown's Battery B, 1st Rhode Island Light Artillery

keyframes.citation: Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492 ('BROWN' named just south of the Copse of Trees; position class, time inferred); 'On July 3rd the four serviceable guns of Brown's Battery were placed just south of the Copse of Trees under the command of Lieutenant Perrin' — Capt. Brown wounded July 2 (Stone Sentinels Rhode Island Battery B page); strength 103 brought to the field, six 12-pdr Napoleons; Lt. Milne detached to Cushing's battery and killed there (Brown 1985 Filson); bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)

wrecked by the converging bombardment, ammunition nearly gone (Brown 1985 Filson; Stone Sentinels: Hunt found the battery severely damaged); losses 7 killed / 19 wounded / 2 missing are whole-battle figures (Stone Sentinels) — decline reconstructed

withdrawn mid-bombardment on Hunt's order, 'an order it obeyed promptly' (Stone Sentinels Rhode Island Battery B page; Brown 1985 Filson) — the withdrawal E. P. Alexander keyed Pickett's advance on (Alexander 1907); time ~14:40 and route inferred
parked in reserve behind the crest (reconstruction)
end: 103 minus 28 whole-battle casualties (Stone Sentinels Rhode Island Battery B page); July-3-only split not documented

Bachelder Third Day sheet, LOC 99447492; posted at Ziegler's Grove, not the Angle proper (Stone Sentinels 1st US Battery I page; Ziegler's Grove anchor OSM node 4497323192); strength 120: Stone Sentinels; bombardment opens 1:00 PM (Alexander 1907) / 1:07 PM (Jacobs 1864)
interpolated: served under the bombardment (Alexander 1907)
Hazard's report (via Iron Brigader): Woodruff fell mortally wounded July 3 during the repulse, died July 4; command to Lt. McCrea; time ~15:25 inferred
end strength 120 minus 25 casualties: Stone Sentinels battery page — two extractions of the source give 25 vs 30 total casualties; conflict flagged, smaller reading used
